Risk: GB/GiB rounding and unit drift in reports; values diverge from canonical bytes
Mitigation: Store and compute ALWAYS in bytes (schema invariant); round only at presentation layer; unit assertions in validation

Risk: Missing AIX golden image / reference standard; nothing to deviate against for rootvg/datavg/JFS2
Mitigation: Build AIX golden image early; layered inheritance (OS-family -> distro -> version -> role); flag hosts with no applicable standard

Risk: Inconsistent naming (VG/LV/mount/host) defeats grouping and standard matching
Mitigation: Normalization + canonical naming dictionary; fuzzy match with human confirmation; record raw + normalized

Risk: False positives/negatives in deviation detection erode trust and hide real risk
Mitigation: Calibrate thresholds per OS/fstype/role; severity tiers; human-in-the-loop on high-impact; precision/recall tracking on labeled set

Risk: LLM hallucination (LangGraph agents invent mounts, standards, or remediations)
Mitigation: Ground every claim in collected JSON; constrained tool outputs; cite snapshot fields; deterministic checks gate LLM narrative

Risk: Broken FS<->topology linkage (group_ref/backing_ref not resolving) breaks storage_class derivation
Mitigation: Referential-integrity validation on every snapshot; orphan-ref alerts; derive storage_class only when links resolve, else mark unknown

Risk: Single-snapshot view: no history -> no growth trend, no forecasting
Mitigation: Persist time-series of snapshots; require >=N points before forecasting; gate objectives 3/4 on history availability

Risk: Privacy / scope creep: collection captures sensitive content beyond metadata
Mitigation: Metadata-only contract; non-sensitive aggregates only; ACL summaries non-sensitive; review collection commands; DLP review

Risk: Command variance across OS versions yields inconsistent or unparseable output
Mitigation: Version-aware command matrix; parser per command/version; collection_method + scan_confidence recorded; fail-soft per field

Risk: Data volume at >=500 hosts x fields x snapshots overwhelms storage/processing
Mitigation: One JSON doc/host; columnar/partitioned store for history; incremental processing; retention policy

Risk: Incomplete collection (host unreachable, partial scan) skews fleet view
Mitigation: scan_confidence + completeness flags; exclude partial from aggregates; retry/backfill; surface coverage metrics

Risk: Reference/standards drift: standards age and diverge from real fleet state
Mitigation: Versioned standards with effective dates; periodic review cadence; drift report standards-vs-fleet

Risk: Validation bottleneck: human review can't keep pace with fleet-scale deviations
Mitigation: Auto-triage by severity; batch low-risk; only high-impact to humans; feedback loop to tune thresholds

Risk: Data-at-rest security of the storage inventory (still infra-sensitive even if non-sensitive content)
Mitigation: Encrypt at rest; access control + audit; least privilege; segregate from production credentials

Risk: OS-landscape change (new distro/version/Windows build) outpaces collectors/standards
Mitigation: Pluggable collectors + standards; onboarding checklist for new OS; backlog SLA for new platforms

Risk: Over-reliance on automation: remediation acted on without human judgment
Mitigation: Human approval gate for changes; recommend-only mode by default; audit trail; clear automation boundaries

Risk: Windows model divergence: no LVM; mapping NTFS volumes / Storage Spaces onto the agnostic schema (pool=group, virtual disk=volume, basic disk=>partitions-as-volumes + empty groups)
Mitigation: Explicit Windows mapping rules with *_kind discriminators; basic-disk path yields empty groups by design; conformance tests on real Windows snapshots; treat C:/BootDevice as system storage_class

Risk: WinRM authentication & security: agentless reach needs credentials, encryption, and trusted transport to >=500 Windows hosts
Mitigation: WinRM over HTTPS/Kerberos; vaulted least-privilege service account; no plaintext creds; per-host scoping; rotate + audit; network segmentation

Risk: BTRFS subvolume/snapshot size accounting: plain df is MISLEADING -> wrong used_percent and wrong forecasts on SLES/SUSE
Mitigation: Use 'btrfs filesystem usage' / qgroups for true bytes (never plain df on btrfs); fstype-aware collector branch; validate against allocated extents

Risk: Agnostic-schema mapping errors: group_ref/backing_ref semantics differ across AIX/Linux/Windows and get crossed
Mitigation: Per-platform mapping spec with key conventions; cross-platform referential-integrity tests; canonical ref namespace; reject ambiguous links

Risk: Standards explosion across OS x distro x version x role (combinatorial standard set)
Mitigation: Layered inheritance (defaults at OS-family, overridden down to role/version); only store deltas; inheritance-resolution tests

Risk: Windows ACL-summary vs Unix mount-options compliance-model mismatch (access_model differs: ntfs-acl vs posix-mount-options)
Mitigation: Two compliance evaluators keyed on access_model; map intent (e.g., no-exec, restricted-write) to per-model checks; never apply mount-option rules to NTFS

Risk: Simultaneous multi-OS delivery (AIX + Linux distros + Windows all first-class, no phase 2) inflates scope and complexity
Mitigation: Shared agnostic schema + pluggable per-OS collectors/standards; parallel workstreams with common contracts; staged enablement behind one schema; explicit MVP per objective on every OS
